creator: peter@FreeBSD.org
text:
The file-a-bug page asks what hardware you're running on.  It's vague, but if somebody reads the help to for a clarification, it says something even worse:

"Hardware
    The hardware platform the bug was observed on. .."

This leads to an endless stream of junk being assigned to the freebsd-amd64 users list., eg:

[Bug 210986] Conflict ZFS with GEOM ?
[Bug 211179] libc strcoll() enters endless loop when comparing two specific UTF8 strings under en_US.UTF-8
[Bug 211195] pw userdel Segmentation fault (core dumped)
[Bug 210142] makewhatis binary is requerd for building release even with WITHOUT_MAN_UTILS=YES
[Bug 211360] ESC characters are not interpreted by /bin/sh


This is just a random sample.  It has basically killed the mailing list.

Please, stop this madness.
